title: And the Angel was SilentSpoiler Alert be advised
display_title: And the Angel was SilentSpoiler Alert be advised
wikitext:
[]: And the Angel was Silent...(Spoiler Alert be advised)             Quote Post by Abaddon » Mon Jul 26, 2004 11:16 pm Warning this topic will cover spoiler material for those who havenâ€™t watched the entire NGE series! The question is simply this, If you could have been in Shinjiâ€™s position at the climax of episode 24, could you have killed Kaworu? I decided not to make this a yes or no forum; because I never have been a fan of â€˜yes or noâ€™ questions. So do you believe you could have killed him? View the situation from different points of view before answering, hindsight is always 20/20 they say. Remember back to when you first watched Kaworuâ€™s demise at that moment, could you have killed him then? Personally, I donâ€™t think I could have killed him. The nature of the angels and their way of thinking is very important. Maybe it was just Kaworuâ€™s angelic nature to seek out and find Adam. After all, the Angels themselves were seeking completion and wholeness through the union of Adam. Humans are the same way, indeed his nature is the same as our own,indeed, can you blame him for being so human? He wished for completion, but saw how hard humans were willing to fight for their future and continued existence, thus in the end faced with utter defeat he willing died so humanity could live.

[]:             Quote Post by coff » Mon Jul 26, 2004 11:20 pm I probably would have killed him. I would have felt like I had to.

[]:             Quote Post by RubberSoul » Mon Jul 26, 2004 11:42 pm I couldn't, Kaworu is just too sweet. I wouldn't have the heart.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Mon Jul 26, 2004 11:58 pm I would have destroyed the seventeenth angel without hesitation. As he said, we need the future. Humanity needs hope to survive, and as long as there is a future there will be hope.

[]:             Quote Post by Xanatose » Tue Jul 27, 2004 12:19 am I love Kaoru. Love him, I say! But in my hands? He'd have gone splat in have the time it took our Hedgehog protagonist. :cry: By the way, nice topic title. :)

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Tue Jul 27, 2004 12:25 am RubberSoul wrote: I couldn't, Kaworu is just too sweet. I wouldn't have the heart.

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Tue Jul 27, 2004 12:29 am I would have. Kaworu = Angel. I'd be thankful for what he did for me, but for the greater good, he's still the enemy and I'd still kill him. But that's just me, and I'm really mean.

[]:             Quote Post by Jason » Tue Jul 27, 2004 1:05 am I would, and then I would have probably have hated myself for it for the rest of life, but with each day the weight of the choice would get lighter until the day where I could let go of it forever.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Tue Jul 27, 2004 3:38 am I never understood how Shinji killed Kaworu.I mean when he had to fight Eva03 with Toji inside it he refused to do it(and he didn't know Toji he was there)because he couldn't kill a child his same age.Then why did he kill Kaworu?

[]:             Quote Post by Asterix » Tue Jul 27, 2004 4:23 am the_seventh_child wrote: I never understood how Shinji killed Kaworu.I mean when he had to fight Eva03 with Toji inside it he refused to do it(and he didn't know Toji he was there)because he couldn't kill a child his same age.Then why did he kill Kaworu?

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Jul 27, 2004 5:19 am in Shinji's position yes...though kaworu would have been the only person to truly return feelings to him...so it would have been really painful... but then again, Asuka would have died...

[]:             Quote Post by Karma Burn » Tue Jul 27, 2004 8:00 am Hell yes! That little bastard had to die! For me, it can be a good day if I can wound the morale of a fangirl. Killing Kaworu is a perfect way to do that. Besides that, he's an angel! Kaworu just looked human. You've got to kill the enemy, don't you?

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Tue Jul 27, 2004 8:04 am Karma Burn wrote: Hell yes! That little bastard had to die!

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Tue Jul 27, 2004 12:21 pm Under pressure, when I can't figure out what to do, I carry out the first orders given to me. Kaworu was an angel, which was the very same type of being I would have been killing the whole series if I had been in Shinji's position. I wouldn't have had much choice but to kill him.

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Tue Jul 27, 2004 1:28 pm Kaworu has always been one of my favorite characters... ...I don't know if I'd be able to do it. I probably would, givin the fact that humanity would end if I didn't.

[]:             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Tue Jul 27, 2004 1:50 pm I'd just remeber that he killed a kitten and BOOM! Or rather SPLAT!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Tue Jul 27, 2004 3:02 pm EVA-01X wrote: I'd just remeber that he killed a kitten and BOOM! Or rather SPLAT!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Tue Jul 27, 2004 3:06 pm Manga 9 he kills a kitten. PFt. And you'd have no trouble killing your best friend?? ; I based my answer on Shinji's point of view. If it were up to me, and Kaworu was just some random Angel and nothing more, then I'd definately just kill him.

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Tue Jul 27, 2004 3:12 pm that bastard! and no, killing a best friend like him would be no big deal for me. although i wouldnt really count him as a best friend only knowing him for a day 1/2

[]:             Quote Post by Quiddity » Tue Jul 27, 2004 4:10 pm Given what would happen with his survival, no question. One can't sacrifice all of humanity because of a friendship with one person. (And given that they were friends for so short a time as I've mentioned in other posts, that makes it even easier, certainly much more so than someone like Touji) In an unbelievable plot twist, me and Dave actually agree on a subject for once.
